video_title: American Manhunt: O.J. Simpson | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
It's my opinion that Nicole threatened O.J.  She received a letter on Monday by courier, immediately called the Beverly Hills realtor who found the condo for her, Jeanne McKenna, and listed the condo, and had Jeanne McKenna drive her around looking at properties, and on Friday signed a one-year lease on a house in Malibu, for 5k/month (but at least it had a pool) (her income was 10k/month child support from O.J.) (notice the kids would have had to change schools, which is probably why when she and O.J. got divorced, she moved to the Gretna Green house, so close to O.J.).  She and Jeanne McKenna were having forbidden cigarettes at the Malibu house after signing and she said, "I can really do this.  I can lease the house and move!"  Then two days later she was murdered.  It's my opinion O.J. sent a letter to the IRS saying Nicole Brown Simpson owes $90,000 because you can't live in a condo you own that is classified as a rental.  If you sell a luxury condo in the San Francisco area, used when O.J. played for the Forty-Niners, then rented out, and buy a luxury condo in Brentwood, you have to rent it out.  Otherwise you have to pay capital gains tax, which was $90,000.  Nicole got the San Francisco condo in the divorce settlement.  It was supposed to provide a place for his kids to live.  She did not receive any alimony, only child support.  I don't know why she didn't understand this concept of capital gains tax.  Probably she thought, "Oh, the IRS will never bother with small fry like me."  But she was probably so angry that right after moving, in February, to Bundy, O.J. would threaten to get her in trouble with the IRS, and make them move, that she threatened to call Frank Olson, the CEO of Hertz, and show him the photos and diary entries in her safe deposit box.  

Nicole's diary entry June 3rd (in Jeffrey Toobin's book):

'You hang up on me last nite, you're gonna pay for this bitch, you're holding money from the IRS, you're going to jail you f--king c--t. You think you can do any f--king thing you want, you've got it comming [sic]--I've already talked to my lawyers about this bitch--they'll get you for tax evasion, bitch, I'll see to it. You're not going to have a dime left bitch' etc."

Christie Prody, O.J.'s girlfriend in Florida for many years, said she thought he murdered Nicole and Ron and he said, "She had it coming."  That interview is on YouTube.

If Frank Olson said we're not going to pay you half a million dollars a year just to play golf with our clients any more, O.J., obviously that is the crux of the matter.

video_title: American Manhunt: O.J. Simpson | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
People sometimes get confused when a network will compile a documentary that discusses a crime that has been covered in every way possible. They often wonder what else could be added to a topic which everyone in the world has become unbelievably well versed. I think it is absolutely crucial that prominent networks continue to air intense reporting on crimes of the past so that viewers who witnessed the media and aftermath initially will one day see the crime through new eyes, new opinions and most importantly, new and improved methods for collecting and processing evidence. What has become so clear when I saw this documentary at 42 as opposed to courtroom footage playing out in real time when I was 13 is how unbelievably unfortunate it is that this case wasnt handled in the best possible way by the best people. I cannot believe how many opportunities were squandered on account of those police and detectives failing to report everything. The disconnect has been so evident that I almost feel as though this crime was doomed to be bungled since the team was dealing with so much more than just crime and punishment. The ego that could have hindered some by not wanting to blamed for mistakes hurt this case significantly. Also, with such a strong and constant media presence the egos belonging to that particular task force were undoubtedly being compromised at a level that none of them could have anticipated.
      The most important take away I have after watching this is the reassurance that had there been any confusion or hesitation in regards to my opinion of OJ Simpson it has dissipated leaving nothing but confidence that he was the only person responsible for the death of Nicole Simpson and Ron Goldman. Also, I wanted to say that as with most high profile crimes the most important element is the loss of lives. Two people who were loved and valued by their friends and family were killed in the most horrific and yet their story is almost an afterthought in what has been hailed the crime of the century aka The OJ Simpson trial. I genuinely hope they are both in a better place with no memory of what they went through. 
    For those who feel as though Oj Simpson never really paid for his crimes against humanity I urge you to look at this case in a different light. When this crime took place Oj Simpson had a huge amount of popularity since he won the hearts of sports fans as well as those who loved watching him in his films. He once said in an interview that money didnt matter so long as he was able to walk down the street seeing people who knew who he was by name. That particular statement of his has so much foreshadowing since it is safe to say that OJ Simpsons name and face is one the majority of the entire world can recognize. The minute he became associated with such a grisly crime that popularity and recognition began to dwindle and by the time the verdict was read, despite being found not guilty, he was very much guilty in terms of public opinion. in my opinion Oj Simpson may as well have gone to prison because he lost so much in the aftermath. With the exception of a few interviews, literary projects and of course his arrest later on for unrelated reasons Oj never gained back any sort of positive notoriety and the golden light he believed to be cast in when he entered a room was completely gone. His friends and other high profile associates no longer wished to be in his company and beautiful women he may have had his pick of dried up. For a person like him, this may have been worse than a jail sentence. it was the very definition of an ego death. I can only imagine what that would do to a person so narcissistic. 
   For those who were not sure if he was responsible I hope this particular doc has given you enough information so you may finalize your opinion.

video_title: American Manhunt: O.J. Simpson | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: What struck me while watching this documentary is that O.J.’s lawyers instrumentalized race to gain sympathy from the Black community while using the ‘white man’s playbook’ to get away with murder—just like in the Rodney King case. Before the trial, O.J. was practically a white man; his remark, ’What are these n*** doing in Brentwood?’ tells you everything you need to know about his disdain for his own community. The lawyers were a disgrace for exploiting the Rodney King case—two injustices don’t make justice. They played the race card but instead proved that with the right resources and backup you can get away with anything. What saddens me the most is that with all the circus surrounding this trial, the victims are forgotten. May they rest in peace.

I don’t know if it’ll be as good as OJ: Made in America, but it certainly will be interesting to see, especially now we can have a new perspective on things since OJ has passed away. Although I still think he was guilty.
